fields.Text:
Patient with HIV, fever, hypoxemia, and diffuse bilateral granular opacities. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pneumocystis jiroveci pneumonia}}
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pneumocystis (carinii) jiroveci pneumonia is the most common cause of interstitial pneumonia in immunocompromised patients. Typical chest radiograph findings include bilaterally diffuse, symmetric, finely granular infiltrates.

fields.Text: In an immunocompromised patient, diffuse symmetric finely granular pulmonary infiltrates are characteristic of {{c1::Pneumocystis jiroveci pneumonia}}.
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pneumocystis (carinii) jiroveci pneumonia is the most common cause of interstitial pneumonia in immunocompromised patients. Typical chest radiograph findings include bilaterally diffuse, symmetric, finely granular infiltrates.

fields.Text:
What MRI artifact is shown?

{{c1::Aliasing (wrap-around artifact)}}
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Aliasing occurs when the field of view (FOV) is too small for the anatomy being imaged. Signals from outside the FOV are then mismapped and wrapped onto the opposite side of the image, due to insufficient data sampling.

fields.Text: MRI wrap-around or aliasing occurs when the {{c1::field of view is too small}} for the imaged anatomy.
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Aliasing occurs when the field of view (FOV) is too small for the anatomy being imaged. Signals from outside the FOV are then mismapped and wrapped onto the opposite side of the image, due to insufficient data sampling.

fields.Text: Complete loss of consciousness, protective reflexes, and response to painful stimuli defines {{c1::general anesthesia}}.
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. General anesthesia provides for a controlled state of unconsciousness in which there is a complete loss of protective reflexes, including the ability to maintain a patent airway and respond to painful stimuli.

fields.Text:
New pacemaker lead has an abnormal arterial course and projects over the left ventricle. What is the complication?

{{c1::Malpositioned AICD/pacemaker lead in the left ventricle}}
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Explanation: The radiograph demonstrates a single lead transvenous pacemaker/AICD device with the lead following an arterial, rather than venous, course.

fields.Text: A pacemaker/AICD lead following an arterial course and projecting over the left ventricle indicates {{c1::malposition in the left ventricle}}.
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Explanation: The radiograph demonstrates a single lead transvenous pacemaker/AICD device with the lead following an arterial, rather than venous, course.

fields.Text:
Child with NF1 and visual disturbance. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Optic glioma}}
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: easy. The image shows isointense enlargement of the optic nerve compared to the contralateral side, which on contrast administration will show avid fusiform enhancement.

fields.Text: In a child with NF1 and visual disturbance, fusiform enlargement of the optic nerve suggests {{c1::optic glioma}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: easy. The image shows isointense enlargement of the optic nerve compared to the contralateral side, which on contrast administration will show avid fusiform enhancement.

fields.Text:
What is the most likely diagnosis of this calcaneal lucent lesion with central calcification?

{{c1::Intraosseous lipoma}}
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Intraosseous lipoma of the calcaneus appears as a well-defined lucent lesion with sclerotic borders on plain radiographs. Appearance varies from purely lucent to mixed lucent-sclerotic with calcifications depending on degree of fat necrosis.

fields.Text: A well-defined lucent calcaneal lesion with central calcification is highly suggestive of {{c1::intraosseous lipoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Intraosseous lipoma of the calcaneus appears as a well-defined lucent lesion with sclerotic borders on plain radiographs. Appearance varies from purely lucent to mixed lucent-sclerotic with calcifications depending on degree of fat necrosis.

fields.Text:
Persistent limb pain and paresthesias after trauma with periarticular uptake on three-phase bone scan. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Complex regional pain syndrome}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Complex regional pain syndrome on three-phase bone scan typically shows increased flow, increased blood pool, and characteristic periarticular uptake in small joints of the affected extremity, though atypical decreased uptake in all phases can occur in late disease or pediatric cases.

fields.Text: Complex regional pain syndrome on three-phase bone scan classically shows {{c1::increased flow, blood pool, and periarticular delayed uptake}} in the affected extremity.
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Complex regional pain syndrome on three-phase bone scan typically shows increased flow, increased blood pool, and characteristic periarticular uptake in small joints of the affected extremity, though atypical decreased uptake in all phases can occur in late disease or pediatric cases.

fields.Text:
Patient with prior pancreatic surgery and upper GI bleeding. What lesion is shown on angiography?

{{c1::Gastroduodenal artery pseudoaneurysm}}
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Post-pancreaticoduodenectomy pseudoaneurysms can be treated with covered stent-grafts or coil embolization. When the feeding vessel is of small size with multiple branches arising from near the pseudoaneurysm (as in this case), coil embolization is preferred over stenting due to inadequate vessel diameter/landing zones and risk of compromising branch vessels.

fields.Text: A gastroduodenal artery pseudoaneurysm after pancreatic surgery with small feeding vessels is generally treated by {{c1::coil embolization}}.
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Post-pancreaticoduodenectomy pseudoaneurysms can be treated with covered stent-grafts or coil embolization. When the feeding vessel is of small size with multiple branches arising from near the pseudoaneurysm (as in this case), coil embolization is preferred over stenting due to inadequate vessel diameter/landing zones and risk of compromising branch vessels.

fields.Text:
Chronic medication exposure with bilateral punctate echogenic renal foci. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Lithium-induced nephropathy}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Bilateral non-shadowing punctate echogenic foci in chronic lithium users represent lithium-induced microcysts that appear echogenic (not anechoic) due to specular reflections from cysts smaller than ultrasound resolution threshold.

fields.Text: Bilateral punctate nonshadowing echogenic renal foci in a chronic lithium user reflect {{c1::lithium-induced microcysts}}.
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Bilateral non-shadowing punctate echogenic foci in chronic lithium users represent lithium-induced microcysts that appear echogenic (not anechoic) due to specular reflections from cysts smaller than ultrasound resolution threshold.

fields.Text: Wheezing or bronchospasm with mild or no hypoxia is classified as a {{c1::moderate allergic-like contrast reaction}}.
fields.Extra: Q10. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: In moderate contrast reactions, signs and symptoms are pronounced and commonly require medical management. Examples of moderate allergic-like reactions include diffuse urticaria or pruritis, diffuse erythema with stable vital signs, facial edema without dyspnea, throat tightness or hoarseness without dyspnea, and wheezing or bronchospasm with mild or no hypoxia.

fields.Text: A contrast reaction with laryngeal edema, stridor, and hypoxia is {{c1::severe}}, whereas wheezing with mild hypoxia is moderate.
fields.Extra: Q10. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: In moderate contrast reactions, signs and symptoms are pronounced and commonly require medical management. Examples of moderate allergic-like reactions include diffuse urticaria or pruritis, diffuse erythema with stable vital signs, facial edema without dyspnea, throat tightness or hoarseness without dyspnea, and wheezing or bronchospasm with mild or no hypoxia.

fields.Text:
What targeting error is present on these stereotactic biopsy images?

{{c1::Combined x-axis and y-axis error}}
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: hard. Pre-fire and post-fire -15 degrees and +15 degrees images are acquired during a stereotactic biopsy.

fields.Text: A stereotactic biopsy needle that is below and lateral to the target on post-fire views has {{c1::combined x-axis and y-axis targeting error}}.
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: hard. Pre-fire and post-fire -15 degrees and +15 degrees images are acquired during a stereotactic biopsy.

fields.Text:
Multiple thin perivascular lung cysts in a patient without LAM. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Lymphocytic interstitial pneumonia}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Lymphocytic interstitial pneumonia shows lower lung-predominant cysts in autoimmune patients, distinguished from Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome by fewer elliptical paramediastinal cysts and from lymphangioleiomyomatosis by non-diffuse distribution.

fields.Text: Multiple thin-walled, lower lung-predominant perivascular cysts are characteristic of {{c1::lymphocytic interstitial pneumonia}}.
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Lymphocytic interstitial pneumonia shows lower lung-predominant cysts in autoimmune patients, distinguished from Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome by fewer elliptical paramediastinal cysts and from lymphangioleiomyomatosis by non-diffuse distribution.

fields.Text:
Patient with a positive Neer test and a hooked acromial osteophyte. Which tendon is impinged?

{{c1::Supraspinatus tendon}}
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Frontal radiograph of the right shoulder shows a prominent hooked osteophyte at the acromial undersurface (blue arrow).

fields.Text: A hooked undersurface acromial osteophyte predisposes to impingement of the {{c1::supraspinatus tendon}}.
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Frontal radiograph of the right shoulder shows a prominent hooked osteophyte at the acromial undersurface (blue arrow).

fields.Text:
What device is shown?

{{c1::Ionization chamber}}
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Ionization chambers, such as the CD V-715 shown, measure radiation by detecting ions created when radiation interacts with gas between two electrodes, producing current proportional to radiation dose.

fields.Text: An ionization chamber measures radiation by detecting {{c1::ions created in gas between two electrodes}}.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Ionization chambers, such as the CD V-715 shown, measure radiation by detecting ions created when radiation interacts with gas between two electrodes, producing current proportional to radiation dose.

fields.Text:
What suspicious mammographic mass margin is shown?

{{c1::Spiculated margin}}
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Spiculated margins on mammography have approximately 95% positive predictive value for malignancy, warranting BI-RADS 5 assessment, and immediate core biopsy

fields.Text: A spiculated mammographic mass is BI-RADS 5, with a malignancy likelihood of {{c1::greater than 95%}}.
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Spiculated margins on mammography have approximately 95% positive predictive value for malignancy, warranting BI-RADS 5 assessment, and immediate core biopsy

fields.Text:
Metastatic breast cancer after therapy: increased bone-scan uptake with increasing sclerosis on CT. What response pattern is shown?

{{c1::Osteoblastic flare phenomenon}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Explanation: Numerous bone metastases are seen on the patient’s prior imaging as multiple foci of tracer uptake on the Tc99m-MDP bone (yellow arrows).

fields.Text: In healing bone metastases, increased post-treatment bone-scan uptake with increasing sclerosis on CT represents {{c1::osteoblastic flare phenomenon}}.
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Explanation: Numerous bone metastases are seen on the patient’s prior imaging as multiple foci of tracer uptake on the Tc99m-MDP bone (yellow arrows).

fields.Text:
Adult with migraines and early strokes with anterior temporal white-matter abnormalities. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::CADASIL}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: moderate. CADASIL is a hereditary small-vessel disease due to mutations in the NOTCH3 gene on chromosome 19, which causes stroke in young to middle-aged adults.

fields.Text: CADASIL is an autosomal dominant small-vessel disease caused by mutations in {{c1::NOTCH3}}.
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: moderate. CADASIL is a hereditary small-vessel disease due to mutations in the NOTCH3 gene on chromosome 19, which causes stroke in young to middle-aged adults.

fields.Text:
What is the most likely diagnosis of the optic nerve lesion with tram-track enhancement?

{{c1::Optic nerve sheath meningioma}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q18. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Tram track enhancement indicating optic nerve sheath meningioma should prompt evaluation for neurofibromatosis type 2, requiring comprehensive central nervous system surveillance.

fields.Text: Tram-track enhancement of the optic nerve sheath suggests meningioma and should prompt evaluation for {{c1::neurofibromatosis type 2}}.
fields.Extra: Q18. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Tram track enhancement indicating optic nerve sheath meningioma should prompt evaluation for neurofibromatosis type 2, requiring comprehensive central nervous system surveillance.

fields.Text: Optic nerve sheath meningioma is associated with {{c1::NF2}}, whereas optic pathway glioma is associated with NF1.
fields.Extra: Q18. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Tram track enhancement indicating optic nerve sheath meningioma should prompt evaluation for neurofibromatosis type 2, requiring comprehensive central nervous system surveillance.

fields.Text:
Low thyroid hormones with diffuse heterogeneous hypervascular thyroid on US. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hashimoto thyroiditis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The micronodular pattern (hypoechoic foci from lymphocytic infiltration) combined with diffuse hypervascularity represents the classic ultrasound appearance of Hashimoto's thyroiditis—simultaneous autoimmune tissue destruction and inflammatory vascular response creating the characteristic heterogeneous, hypervascular thyroid pattern.

fields.Text: A heterogeneous micronodular thyroid with diffuse hypervascularity in hypothyroidism is characteristic of {{c1::Hashimoto thyroiditis}}.
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The micronodular pattern (hypoechoic foci from lymphocytic infiltration) combined with diffuse hypervascularity represents the classic ultrasound appearance of Hashimoto's thyroiditis—simultaneous autoimmune tissue destruction and inflammatory vascular response creating the characteristic heterogeneous, hypervascular thyroid pattern.

fields.Text:
Restaging PET/CT shows symmetric supraclavicular and paraspinal FDG uptake corresponding to fat. What is this finding?

{{c1::Brown fat}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: easy. The PET images and fused PET CT demonstrate symmetric increased FDG uptake in the supraclavicular regions, midaxillary line, and paraspinal regions in the posterior mediastinum (red arrows).

fields.Text: Symmetric FDG-avid supraclavicular, axillary, or paraspinal fat on PET/CT represents metabolically active {{c1::brown fat}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: easy. The PET images and fused PET CT demonstrate symmetric increased FDG uptake in the supraclavicular regions, midaxillary line, and paraspinal regions in the posterior mediastinum (red arrows).

fields.Text:
3-year-old with rigidity and an eye-of-the-tiger sign on MRI. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pantothenate kinase-associated neurodegeneration (PKAN)}}
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Eye-of-the-tiger sign in a 3-year-old is pathognomonic for PKAN; this is around the earliest age when this characteristic sign develops.

fields.Text: The eye-of-the-tiger sign in a young child is characteristic of {{c1::pantothenate kinase-associated neurodegeneration (PKAN)}}.
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Eye-of-the-tiger sign in a 3-year-old is pathognomonic for PKAN; this is around the earliest age when this characteristic sign develops.

fields.Text:
What congenital pulmonary venous anomaly produces the curved opacity shown on this radiograph?

{{c1::Scimitar syndrome}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hypogenetic lung syndrome (scimitar syndrome) features a hypoplastic right lung with anomalous pulmonary venous drainage to the inferior vena cava, creating the characteristic curved scimitar sign opacity on chest radiography representing the anomalous vein coursing toward the diaphragm.

fields.Text: Scimitar syndrome consists of a hypoplastic right lung with anomalous pulmonary venous drainage to the {{c1::inferior vena cava}}.
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hypogenetic lung syndrome (scimitar syndrome) features a hypoplastic right lung with anomalous pulmonary venous drainage to the inferior vena cava, creating the characteristic curved scimitar sign opacity on chest radiography representing the anomalous vein coursing toward the diaphragm.

fields.Text: Fluoroscopic magnification increases peak skin dose, but leaves stochastic cancer risk approximately unchanged because {{c1::dose-area product remains approximately unchanged}}.
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Magnification keeps DAP and stochastic cancer risk unchanged because increased dose rate is offset by proportionally smaller field area, maintaining constant total energy imparted. However, deterministic skin injury risk increases due to higher concentrated dose per unit area.

fields.Text: At administration, Mo-99 breakthrough in Tc-99m must not exceed {{c1::0.15 microCi per mCi of Tc-99m}}.
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In the production of Tc99m, radionuclide purity must be demonstrated by showing that the fraction 99Mo does not exceed 0.15 μCi per mCi of 99mTc at time of administration.

fields.Text:
Pain below the mandible with an echogenic focus and upstream ductal dilation on US. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Left Wharton duct sialolithiasis}}
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Echogenic focus with posterior shadowing in Wharton's duct plus upstream dilatation indicates sialolithiasis. Per the literature, 70% to 95% of salivary stones occur in the submandibular gland, with 65% showing associated ductal dilatation. The mylohyoid muscle turn is a common impaction site.

fields.Text: An echogenic focus with posterior shadowing in Wharton duct plus upstream dilation indicates {{c1::submandibular sialolithiasis}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Echogenic focus with posterior shadowing in Wharton's duct plus upstream dilatation indicates sialolithiasis. Per the literature, 70% to 95% of salivary stones occur in the submandibular gland, with 65% showing associated ductal dilatation. The mylohyoid muscle turn is a common impaction site.

fields.Text:
Solid parotid mass in a young adult. What is the leading diagnosis?

{{c1::Pleomorphic adenoma}}
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: US-guided FNA has high specificity for malignancy detection. Detection of malignant cytology is crucial for treatment planning, given increased rates of upfront neck dissection and wider surgical margins in such cases. Core needle biopsy should be performed when FNA is inadequate or nondiagnostic.

fields.Text: A solid parotid mass is typically sampled initially with {{c1::ultrasound-guided fine needle aspiration}} to guide treatment planning.
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: US-guided FNA has high specificity for malignancy detection. Detection of malignant cytology is crucial for treatment planning, given increased rates of upfront neck dissection and wider surgical margins in such cases. Core needle biopsy should be performed when FNA is inadequate or nondiagnostic.

fields.Text:
Chest pain with patchy subepicardial delayed enhancement in the inferolateral wall. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Acute myocarditis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Myocarditis demonstrates characteristic subepicardial or mid-wall late gadolinium enhancement in a non-coronary artery distribution, most commonly affecting the basal inferolateral wall.

fields.Text: Acute myocarditis classically shows {{c1::subepicardial or mid-wall late gadolinium enhancement in a noncoronary distribution}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Myocarditis demonstrates characteristic subepicardial or mid-wall late gadolinium enhancement in a non-coronary artery distribution, most commonly affecting the basal inferolateral wall.

fields.Text:
What named fracture is shown?

{{c1::Bennett fracture}}
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Bennett fracture is a non-comminuted intra-articular fracture of the base of the first metacarpal. Rolando fracture is its comminuted counterpart.

fields.Text: A noncomminuted intra-articular fracture at the base of the first metacarpal is a {{c1::Bennett fracture}}.
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Bennett fracture is a non-comminuted intra-articular fracture of the base of the first metacarpal. Rolando fracture is its comminuted counterpart.

fields.Text:
Fatty thickening of the interatrial septum that spares the fossa ovalis. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Lipomatous hypertrophy of the interatrial septum}}
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Lipomatous hypertrophy of the interatrial septum is characterized by nonencapsulated fat deposition within the interatrial septum, producing a fatty lesion (blue arrow) that follows fat density on computed tomography (CT) and fat signal on magnetic resonance (MR) sequences (hyperintense on T1WI and hypointense on fat-s

fields.Text: Fatty thickening of the interatrial septum that spares the fossa ovalis is {{c1::lipomatous hypertrophy of the interatrial septum}}.
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Lipomatous hypertrophy of the interatrial septum is characterized by nonencapsulated fat deposition within the interatrial septum, producing a fatty lesion (blue arrow) that follows fat density on computed tomography (CT) and fat signal on magnetic resonance (MR) sequences (hyperintense on T1WI and hypointense on fat-s

fields.Text:
What suspicious mammographic mass margin is shown?

{{c1::Spiculated margin}}
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Spiculated margins on mammography are highly suspicious for malignancy, representing radiating tumor extensions that require immediate recall and tissue sampling as they strongly indicate invasive breast carcinoma.

fields.Text: Lines radiating from the margin of a mammographic mass define a {{c1::spiculated margin}}.
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Spiculated margins on mammography are highly suspicious for malignancy, representing radiating tumor extensions that require immediate recall and tissue sampling as they strongly indicate invasive breast carcinoma.

fields.Text: A mass with radiating lines from its margin is described as {{c1::spiculated}}, not merely indistinct.
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Spiculated margins on mammography are highly suspicious for malignancy, representing radiating tumor extensions that require immediate recall and tissue sampling as they strongly indicate invasive breast carcinoma.

fields.Text: The cyclotron-produced radionuclide with a 20-minute half-life is {{c1::carbon-11}}.
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Carbon-11 is cyclotron-produced with a 20-minute half-life, requiring chemical laboratories adjacent to cyclotrons for rapid radiopharmaceutical synthesis due to its short decay time.

fields.Text:
Episodic hypertension with a heterogeneous hemorrhagic adrenal mass. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pheochromocytoma}}
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: easy. The images demonstrate a heterogeneous mass seen on the T1-weighted image (red arrow), which on T2-weighted images appears heterogeneous, with internal pockets of hemorrhage seen as fluid-fluid levels on the T2-weighted images (green arrows).

fields.Text: In a patient with episodic hypertension and an adrenal mass concerning for pheochromocytoma, the key confirmatory test is {{c1::urine metanephrines}}.
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: easy. The images demonstrate a heterogeneous mass seen on the T1-weighted image (red arrow), which on T2-weighted images appears heterogeneous, with internal pockets of hemorrhage seen as fluid-fluid levels on the T2-weighted images (green arrows).

fields.Text:
Diffuse low T1 marrow signal relative to skeletal muscle. What abnormality is present?

{{c1::Bone marrow infiltration}}
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Normal bone marrow appears hyperintense to skeletal muscle on T1-weighted imaging due to fat content. Bone marrow infiltration appears isointense or hypointense to skeletal muscle on T1 because pathologic processes replace the normal fatty marrow elements. This loss of fat signal is the key finding that distinguishes abnormal from normal marrow on T1-weighted sequences.

fields.Text: Marrow is abnormal on nonfat-suppressed T1 imaging when it becomes isointense or hypointense to skeletal muscle because of {{c1::bone marrow infiltration}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Normal bone marrow appears hyperintense to skeletal muscle on T1-weighted imaging due to fat content. Bone marrow infiltration appears isointense or hypointense to skeletal muscle on T1 because pathologic processes replace the normal fatty marrow elements. This loss of fat signal is the key finding that distinguishes abnormal from normal marrow on T1-weighted sequences.

fields.Text:
What is the most likely diagnosis of these thin-walled anechoic renal lesions with posterior enhancement?

{{c1::Simple renal cortical cysts}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Simple renal cysts appear as thin-walled anechoic structures with posterior acoustic enhancement on ultrasound. These benign lesions comprise >70% of incidental renal masses.

fields.Text: Simple renal cysts appear on ultrasound as {{c1::thin-walled anechoic lesions with posterior acoustic enhancement}}.
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Simple renal cysts appear as thin-walled anechoic structures with posterior acoustic enhancement on ultrasound. These benign lesions comprise >70% of incidental renal masses.

fields.Text:
VP shunt study shows tracer stopping in the lower thorax without reaching the peritoneum. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Lower thoracic VP shunt obstruction}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q35. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Lower thoracic shunt obstruction shows tracer flowing normally from reservoir but stopping abruptly at thoracic level without reaching peritoneum, indicating mid-shunt mechanical blockage requiring surgical revision of that tubing segment. Tc-99m pertechnetate has come into favor for shunt studies; its shorter half-life is usually sufficient in these cases.

fields.Text: On a VP shunt study, radiotracer that stops in the lower thorax without reaching the peritoneum indicates {{c1::lower thoracic shunt obstruction}}.
fields.Extra: Q35. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Lower thoracic shunt obstruction shows tracer flowing normally from reservoir but stopping abruptly at thoracic level without reaching peritoneum, indicating mid-shunt mechanical blockage requiring surgical revision of that tubing segment. Tc-99m pertechnetate has come into favor for shunt studies; its shorter half-life is usually sufficient in these cases.

fields.Text: On a shunt study, tracer stopping in the thoracic catheter without peritoneal dispersion indicates {{c1::mechanical shunt obstruction}}, not a normal study.
fields.Extra: Q35. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Lower thoracic shunt obstruction shows tracer flowing normally from reservoir but stopping abruptly at thoracic level without reaching peritoneum, indicating mid-shunt mechanical blockage requiring surgical revision of that tubing segment. Tc-99m pertechnetate has come into favor for shunt studies; its shorter half-life is usually sufficient in these cases.

fields.Text:
Painful groin mass after cardiac catheterization with the US appearance shown. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Iatrogenic femoral artery pseudoaneurysm}}
fields.Extra: Q36. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The ultrasound shows an iatrogenic femoral artery pseudoaneurysm.

fields.Text: Percutaneous US-guided treatment of an iatrogenic femoral artery pseudoaneurysm uses thrombin, commonly in the range of {{c1::100 to 1000 IU}}.
fields.Extra: Q36. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The ultrasound shows an iatrogenic femoral artery pseudoaneurysm.

fields.Text: The usual US-guided thrombin injection dose range for a femoral pseudoaneurysm is {{c1::100 to 1000 IU}}; 2000 IU is high.
fields.Extra: Q36. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The ultrasound shows an iatrogenic femoral artery pseudoaneurysm.

fields.Text:
18-month-old with a cystic cerebellar mass and enhancing mural nodule. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pilocytic astrocytoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Pilocytic astrocytoma classically appears as a cystic mass with intensely enhancing mural nodule. Minimal vasogenic edema is present. The solid component is hypointense on T1-weighted and heterogeneous/hyperintense on T2-weighted images.

fields.Text: A cystic cerebellar mass with an intensely enhancing mural nodule is classic for {{c1::pilocytic astrocytoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Pilocytic astrocytoma classically appears as a cystic mass with intensely enhancing mural nodule. Minimal vasogenic edema is present. The solid component is hypointense on T1-weighted and heterogeneous/hyperintense on T2-weighted images.

fields.Text: Bilateral neuropathic shoulder arthropathy should prompt evaluation for cervical syrinx with {{c1::MRI of the cervical spine}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: easy. Bilateral neuropathic arthropathy of the shoulders has been described in the setting of syrinx formation in the cervical spine.

fields.Text:
What functional thyroid nodule pattern is shown on this I-123 scan?

{{c1::Autonomous hyperfunctioning nodule}}
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Autonomous functioning nodules appear as solitary hot areas with suppressed background thyroid and have very low malignancy risk, rarely requiring FNA unless ultrasound features are concerning.

fields.Text: An autonomous hyperfunctioning thyroid nodule is a solitary hot nodule with suppressed background thyroid uptake and {{c1::very low malignancy risk}}.
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Autonomous functioning nodules appear as solitary hot areas with suppressed background thyroid and have very low malignancy risk, rarely requiring FNA unless ultrasound features are concerning.

fields.Text:
Smooth isoechoic thyroid nodule with a hypoechoic halo. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Follicular adenoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Isoechoic appearance with smooth margins favors follicular adenoma over carcinoma. Isoechogenicity is a predictor of benignity, reflecting preserved follicular architecture in adenomas. Hypoechogenicity is more characteristic of follicular carcinoma than adenoma.

fields.Text: A smooth, isoechoic thyroid nodule with a hypoechoic halo favors {{c1::follicular adenoma}} over carcinoma.
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Isoechoic appearance with smooth margins favors follicular adenoma over carcinoma. Isoechogenicity is a predictor of benignity, reflecting preserved follicular architecture in adenomas. Hypoechogenicity is more characteristic of follicular carcinoma than adenoma.

fields.Text: In fluoroscopic automatic brightness control, lower patient dose is achieved by {{c1::increasing kVp while decreasing mA}}.
fields.Extra: Q41. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Higher kVp creates more penetrating photons that traverse rather than absorb in patient tissues, requiring fewer total photons (lower mAs) to achieve equivalent detector exposure, resulting in lower overall patient dose through improved beam efficiency.

fields.Text: For fluoroscopy ABC, increasing kVp while reducing mA lowers dose at the expense of {{c1::reduced image contrast}}.
fields.Extra: Q41. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Higher kVp creates more penetrating photons that traverse rather than absorb in patient tissues, requiring fewer total photons (lower mAs) to achieve equivalent detector exposure, resulting in lower overall patient dose through improved beam efficiency.

fields.Text:
What type of thyroid nodule is shown on this I-123 scan?

{{c1::Cold thyroid nodule}}
fields.Extra: Q42. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Greater than 85–90% of thyroid nodules are cold (hypo-functional) on thyroid scintigraphy. The majority have benign etiologies, such as cysts, colloid nodules, or thyroiditis. Some patients with cold nodules have a malignancy. The incidence of cancer in a cold thyroid nodule ranges from 5-20%, depending on source.

fields.Text: The risk of malignancy in a cold thyroid nodule is approximately {{c1::5-20%}}.
fields.Extra: Q42. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Greater than 85–90% of thyroid nodules are cold (hypo-functional) on thyroid scintigraphy. The majority have benign etiologies, such as cysts, colloid nodules, or thyroiditis. Some patients with cold nodules have a malignancy. The incidence of cancer in a cold thyroid nodule ranges from 5-20%, depending on source.

fields.Text: A cold thyroid nodule is usually benign but carries a malignancy risk of approximately {{c1::5-20%}}, not 1%.
fields.Extra: Q42. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Greater than 85–90% of thyroid nodules are cold (hypo-functional) on thyroid scintigraphy. The majority have benign etiologies, such as cysts, colloid nodules, or thyroiditis. Some patients with cold nodules have a malignancy. The incidence of cancer in a cold thyroid nodule ranges from 5-20%, depending on source.

fields.Text: A pregnant radiation worker must {{c1::declare the pregnancy in writing voluntarily}} for fetal dose monitoring to apply.
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A pregnant worker can voluntarily inform their employer, in writing, of the pregnancy and the estimated date of conception for proper dose monitoring.

fields.Text:
What normal venous Doppler finding is demonstrated by this waveform?

{{c1::Cardiorespiratory phasicity}}
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The waveform demonstrates cardiorespiratory variability in venous flow through the imaged region.

fields.Text: Cardiorespiratory variability in a lower-extremity venous Doppler waveform indicates {{c1::patency of veins central to the sampled location}}.
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The waveform demonstrates cardiorespiratory variability in venous flow through the imaged region.

fields.Text: Venous cardiorespiratory phasicity indicates {{c1::central venous patency}}, not normal venous valve function.
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The waveform demonstrates cardiorespiratory variability in venous flow through the imaged region.

fields.Text:
What radiation detection device is shown?

{{c1::Ionization detector}}
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Ionization chambers have a distinctive gas-filled electrode chamber (cylindrical or box-like housing). Geiger-Mueller counters have the characteristic "pancake probe"—a thin, flat, circular detector window for alpha/beta detection.

fields.Text: An ionization detector is less sensitive but more accurate than a {{c1::Geiger-Mueller detector}} for radiation survey measurement.
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Ionization chambers have a distinctive gas-filled electrode chamber (cylindrical or box-like housing). Geiger-Mueller counters have the characteristic "pancake probe"—a thin, flat, circular detector window for alpha/beta detection.

fields.Text:
Acute abdominal pain with nonenhancing pancreatic parenchyma. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Necrotizing pancreatitis}}
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: easy. The imaging findings in this case are most consistent with necrotizing pancreatitis.

fields.Text: The most important imaging prognostic factor in acute pancreatitis is the {{c1::degree of pancreatic nonenhancement/necrosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: easy. The imaging findings in this case are most consistent with necrotizing pancreatitis.

fields.Text:
Echogenic adnexal mass with dirty shadowing. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Mature teratoma (dermoid cyst)}}
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: T1-weighted fat-saturated sequences are essential for mature teratoma diagnosis, demonstrating signal dropout that confirms fat content and distinguishes it from hemorrhage.

fields.Text: Signal dropout on T1 fat-saturated imaging confirms the macroscopic fat of a {{c1::mature teratoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: T1-weighted fat-saturated sequences are essential for mature teratoma diagnosis, demonstrating signal dropout that confirms fat content and distinguishes it from hemorrhage.

fields.Text:
Child with chronic headaches and caudal displacement of the cerebellar tonsils. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Chiari I malformation}}
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. This sagittal image shows displacement of cerebellar tonsil below the upper margin of the foramen magnum, which is a characteristic of Chiari I malformation, the most common variant of the Chiari malformations.

fields.Text: Caudal displacement of the cerebellar tonsils through the foramen magnum is characteristic of {{c1::Chiari I malformation}}.
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. This sagittal image shows displacement of cerebellar tonsil below the upper margin of the foramen magnum, which is a characteristic of Chiari I malformation, the most common variant of the Chiari malformations.

fields.Text:
Painless proptosis with symmetric enlargement of the medial and inferior rectus muscles. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Thyroid eye disease}}
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: easy. The image demonstrates a symmetric enlargement of the medial and inferior extra-ocular muscles as well as the superior rectus muscles.

fields.Text: Symmetric enlargement of the inferior and medial rectus muscles with painless proptosis suggests {{c1::thyroid eye disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: easy. The image demonstrates a symmetric enlargement of the medial and inferior extra-ocular muscles as well as the superior rectus muscles.

fields.Text:
What is the most likely diagnosis of this fat-containing adnexal mass?

{{c1::Mature ovarian teratoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Ovarian teratomas are characteristically bright on both in-phase and out-of-phase imaging.

fields.Text: Macroscopic fat in an ovarian teratoma remains bright on both in-phase and out-of-phase imaging because {{c1::pure fat does not undergo opposed-phase signal cancellation}}.
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Ovarian teratomas are characteristically bright on both in-phase and out-of-phase imaging.

fields.Text: Absence of out-of-phase signal dropout does not exclude macroscopic fat; a mature ovarian teratoma can remain {{c1::bright on both in-phase and out-of-phase images}}.
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Ovarian teratomas are characteristically bright on both in-phase and out-of-phase imaging.
